canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, PRM800K is characterized as a dataset used to assess mathematical reasoning. The definitions describe its contents in complementary ways; some papers frame it broadly as containing "manually annotated step-by-step feedback on mathematical reasoning problems," while another provides more specific details, noting it "consists of 80,000 incorrect stepwise student solutions... each marked at the first error step." The problems themselves are sourced from the MATH dataset, which contains challenging competition-level math problems. The most common application described is for training models, specifically "process-supervised reward models" or a "step-level PRM." In line with its content and origin, the dataset is explicitly used to measure the construct of Mathematical reasoning.
papers: AIMMerging: Adaptive Iterative Model Merging Using Training Trajectories for Language Model Continual Learning
Feature Extraction and Steering for Enhanced Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Language Models
ImpliRet: Benchmarking the Implicit Fact Retrieval Challenge
Discriminative Policy Optimization for Token-Level Reward Models
Understanding Chain-of-Thought in LLMs through Information Theory

definitions.text: A dataset containing manually annotated step-by-step feedback on mathematical reasoning problems, used for training process-supervised reward models.
definitions.paper: Discriminative Policy Optimization for Token-Level Reward Models

relationships.paper: ImpliRet: Benchmarking the Implicit Fact Retrieval Challenge
relationships.evidence: PRM800K (Lightman et al., 2024) is derived from the MATH dataset (Hendrycks et al., 2021), which contains challenging competition-level math problems. (Section 4.2)

emnlp25: ImpliRet: Benchmarking the Implicit Fact Retrieval Challenge
